mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls raises aggregate wage income and consumption expenditure, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window or positive 20-day return frequency falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 5% reflects restrictive monetary policy that elevates discount rates and debt-service costs, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month as equity valuations compress.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously elevated consumer prices and a high federal funds rate generate stagflationary pressure that raises real discount rates while squeezing profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
regime_hint: high inflation with restrictive monetary policy
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in fewer than 50% of instances when both proxies meet trigger conditions.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during late-cycle expansions signals above-trend capacity utilization and earnings momentum, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are not positive in greater than 55% of late-cycle months with INDPRO acceleration.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in retail sales indicates stronger-than-expected consumer demand that lifts near-term revenue forecasts for consumer-facing firms, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next trading week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of months when RSAFS increases.

mechanism_narrative: A cumulative rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor income and falling aggregate demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered periods despite rising unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in housing starts reflects resilient credit conditions and construction activity that support cyclical sector earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next trading week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of months when HOUST rises.

mechanism_narrative: A declining consumer price index over a three-month window reduces inflation uncertainty and stabilizes real discount rates, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as equity risk premiums fall.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns during disinflationary windows are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
